gpt55_review_summary: GPT-5.5 high-reasoning review found no blocking issues and identified two residual risks: databricks_runs.py still hardcoded the g6 prefix map and direct validator imports could change public __module__ identity. The PR was patched to route databricks_runs.py through _hardware_targets.py, preserve public wrapper module identities in benchmarks.py and databricks_job.py, and add focused regression tests. Delta review approved with no findings.
scope: src/document_kv_cache/_hardware_targets.py; src/document_kv_cache/benchmarks.py; src/document_kv_cache/databricks_job.py; src/document_kv_cache/databricks_runs.py; tests/test_benchmarks.py; tests/test_databricks_job.py; tests/test_databricks_runs.py; docs/v1-requirements-matrix.md; pr-evidence/centralize-hardware-targets
verification: PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_benchmarks.py::test_benchmark_suite_defaults_to_v1_contract tests/test_databricks_job.py::test_generic_single_node_gpu_aliases_preserve_g5_compatibility_names tests/test_databricks_runs.py::test_databricks_run_status_uses_shared_hardware_target_prefixes -q (3 passed); PYTHONPATH=src pytest tests/test_benchmarks.py tests/test_databricks_job.py tests/test_databricks_runs.py tests/test_public_package.py tests/test_project_governance.py -q (169 passed); python -m compileall -q src tests; git diff --check; PYTHONPATH=src pytest -q (1381 passed)
what_changed: Centralized the V1 hardware-target identifier, display name, default Databricks node type, and accepted Databricks node-type prefixes in a private hardware-target profile, then wired benchmark defaults, Databricks job defaults, and Databricks run-status validation to that shared profile while preserving legacy public aliases.
why: V1 hardware-target metadata was duplicated across benchmark and Databricks modules, making future hardware-target changes easy to apply inconsistently. Centralizing the profile keeps the current aws-g6-l4 behavior unchanged while making later g5/g6 target changes safer and auditable.
